H3500.strongs_def: Jether, the name of five or six Israelites and of one Midianite

H3503.derivation: from H3499 (יֶתֶר) with pron. suffix; his excellence;

H3505.strongs_def: a Jithrite or descendant of Jether

H3506.strongs_def: Jithran, the name of an Edomite and of an Israelite

H3507.derivation: from H3499 (יֶתֶר) and H5971 (עַם); excellence of people;

H3508.strongs_def: the lobe or flap of the liver (as if redundant or outhanging)

H3510.strongs_def: properly, to feel pain; by implication, to grieve; figuratively, to spoil
H3510.kjv_def: grieving, mar, have pain, make sad (sore), (be) sorrowful.

H3513.strongs_def: to be heavy, i.e. in a bad sense (burdensome, severe, dull) or in a good sense (numerous, rich, honorable; causatively, to make weighty (in the same two senses)
H3513.kjv_def: abounding with, more grievously afflict, boast, be chargeable, [idiom] be dim, glorify, be (make) glorious (things), glory, (very) great, be grievous, harden, be (make) heavy, be heavier, lay heavily, (bring to, come to, do, get, be had in) honour (self), (be) honourable (man), lade, [idiom] more be laid, make self many, nobles, prevail, promote (to honour), be rich, be (go) sore, stop.
H3513.usage: honour(34x), glorify(14x), honourable(14x), heavy(13x), harden(7x), glorious(5x), sore(3x), chargeable(2x), great(2x), many(2x), heavier(2x), promote(2x)

H3515.strongs_def: heavy; figuratively in a good sense (numerous) or in a bad sense (severe, difficult, stupid)
H3515.kjv_def: (so) great, grievous, hard(-ened), (too) heavy(-ier), laden, much, slow, sore, thick.
H3515.usage: great(8x), grievous(8x), heavy(8x), sore(4x), hard(2x), much(2x), slow(2x), hardened(1x), heavier(1x), laden(1x), thick(1x)

H3516.strongs_def: the liver (as the heaviest of the viscera)

H3518.strongs_def: to expire or (causatively) to extinguish (fire, light, anger)

H3519.strongs_def: properly, weight, but only figuratively in a good sense, splendor or copiousness

H3520.derivation: irregular feminine passive participle of H3513 (כָּבַד);

H3521.derivation: from the same as H3525 (כֶּבֶל) in the sense of limitation; sterile;
H3521.strongs_def: Cabul, the name of two places in Palestine

H3522.derivation: from an unused root meaning to heap up; hilly;

H3523.derivation: from H3527 (כָּבַר) in the original sense of plaiting;

H3524.strongs_def: vast, whether in extent (figuratively, of power, mighty; of time, aged), or in number, many
H3524.kjv_def: [phrase] feeble, mighty, most, much, strong, valiant.

H3525.derivation: from an unused root meaning to twine or braid together;

H3526.strongs_def: to trample; hence, to wash (properly, by stamping with the feet), whether literal (including the fulling process) or figurative

H3527.strongs_def: properly, to plait together, i.e. (figuratively) to augment (especially in number or quantity, to accumulate)

H3528.strongs_def: properly, extent of time, i.e. a great while; hence, long ago, formerly, hitherto

H3530.strongs_def: properly, length, i.e. a measure (of uncertain dimension)

H3531.derivation: from H3527 (כָּבַר) in its original sense;

H3532.derivation: from an unused root meaning to dominate;
H3532.strongs_def: a ram (just old enough to butt)

H3533.strongs_def: to tread down; hence, negatively, to disregard; positively, to conquer, subjugate, violate
H3533.kjv_def: bring into bondage, force, keep under, subdue, bring into subjection.

H3537.derivation: from an unused root meaning to deepen;
H3537.strongs_def: properly, a pail; but generally of earthenware; a jar  for domestic purposes

H3538.derivation: (Aramaic) from a root corresponding to H3576 (כָּזַב);

H3539.derivation: from the same as H3537 (כַּד) in the sense of striking fire from a metal forged;
H3539.strongs_def: a sparkling gem, probably the ruby

H3541.derivation: from the prefix k and H1931 (הוּא);
H3541.strongs_def: properly, like this, i.e. by implication, (of manner) thus (or so); also (of place) here (or hither); or (of time) now
H3541.kjv_def: also, here, + hitherto, like, on the other side, so (and much), such, on that manner, (on) this (manner, side, way, way and that way), + mean while, yonder.

H3542.strongs_def: {properly, like this, i.e. by implication, (of manner) thus (or so); also (of place) here (or hither); or (of time) now}

H3543.strongs_def: to be weak, i.e. (figuratively) to despond (causatively, rebuke), or (of light, the eye) to grow dull
H3543.kjv_def: darken, be dim, fail, faint, restrain, [idiom] utterly.
H3543.usage: dim(3x), fail(1x), faint(1x), darkened(1x), utterly(1x), restrained(1x)

H3544.kjv_def: somewhat dark, darkish, wax dim, heaviness, smoking.

H3545.strongs_def: properly, a weakening; figuratively, alleviation, i.e. cure

H3546.derivation: (Aramaic) a root corresponding to and H3557 (כּוּל);

H3547.derivation: a primitive root, apparently meaning to mediate in religious services; but used only as denominative from H3548 (כֹּהֵן);
H3547.strongs_def: to officiate as a priest; figuratively, to put on regalia
H3547.kjv_def: deck, be (do the office of a, execute the, minister in the) priest('s office).

H3548.strongs_def: literally one officiating, a priest; also (by courtesy) an acting priest (although a layman)
H3548.kjv_def: chief ruler, [idiom] own, priest, prince, principal officer.

H3549.strongs_def: {one officiating, a priest; also (by courtesy) an acting priest (although a layman)}

H3551.derivation: (Aramaic) from a root corresponding to H3854 (לַהַג) in the sense of piercing;

H3553.derivation: from an unused root meaning to be high or rounded;

H3554.strongs_def: properly, to prick or penetrate; hence, to blister (as smarting or eating into)

H3556.derivation: probably from the same as H3522 (כַּבּוֹן) (in the sense of rolling) or H3554 (כָּוָה) (in the sense of blazing);
H3556.strongs_def: a star (as round or as shining); figuratively, a prince

H3557.strongs_def: properly, to keep in; hence, to measure; figuratively, to maintain (in various senses)
H3557.kjv_def: (be able to, can) abide, bear, comprehend, contain, feed, forbearing, guide, hold(-ing in), nourish(-er), be present, make provision, receive, sustain, provide sustenance (victuals).
H3557.usage: contain(6x), feed(6x), sustain(4x), abide(3x), nourish(3x), hold(2x), receive(2x), victual(2x), bear(1x), comprehended(1x)

H3558.derivation: from an unused root meaning to store away;

H3559.strongs_def: properly, to be erect (i.e. stand perpendicular); hence (causatively) to set up, in a great variety of applications, whether literal (establish, fix, prepare, apply), or figurative (appoint, render sure, proper or prosperous)
H3559.kjv_def: certain(-ty), confirm, direct, faithfulness, fashion, fasten, firm, be fitted, be fixed, frame, be meet, ordain, order, perfect, (make) preparation, prepare (self), provide, make provision, (be, make) ready, right, set (aright, fast, forth), be stable, (e-) stablish, stand, tarry, [idiom] very deed.
H3559.usage: prepare(85x), establish(58x), ready(17x), stablish(5x), provide(5x), right(5x), fixed(4x), set(4x), direct(3x), order(3x), fashion(3x), variant(2x), certain(2x), confirmed(2x), firm(2x), preparation(2x)

H3561.strongs_def: something prepared, i.e. a sacrificial wafer

H3562.derivation: from H3559 (כּוּן) and H3050 (יָהּ); Jah has sustained;
H3562.strongs_def: Conanjah, the name of two Israelites

H3563.derivation: from an unused root meaning to hold together;
H3563.strongs_def: a cup (as a container), often figuratively, a lot (as if a potion); also some unclean bird, probably an owl (perhaps from the cup-like cavity of its eye)

H3564.derivation: from an unused root meaning properly, to dig through;
H3564.strongs_def: a pot or furnace (as if excavated)

H3565.derivation: from H3564 (כּוּר) and H6227 (עָשָׁן); furnace of smoke;

H3566.derivation: or (Ezra 1:1 (last time),2) כֹּרֶשׁ; from the Persians;

H3568.strongs_def: Cush (or Ethiopia), the name of a son of Ham, and of his territory; also of an Israelite
H3568.pos: proper locative noun, proper masculine noun

H3569.strongs_def: a Cushite, or descendant of Cush

H3570.strongs_def: Cushi, the name of two Israelites

H3573.derivation: apparently from H3572 (כּוּשָׁן) and the dual of H7564 (רִשְׁעָה); Cushan of double wickedness;

H3575.strongs_def: Cuth or Cuthah, a province of Assyria

H3576.strongs_def: to lie (i.e. deceive), literally or figuratively
H3576.kjv_def: fail, (be found a, make a) liar, lie, lying, be in vain.

H3577.kjv_def: deceitful, false, leasing, + liar, lie, lying.
H3577.usage: lie(23x), lying(2x), leasing(2x), deceitful(1x), false(1x), liar(1x)

H3581.derivation: or (Daniel 11:6) כּוֹחַ; from an unused root meaning to be firm;
H3581.strongs_def: vigor, literally (force, in a good or a bad sense) or figuratively (capacity, means, produce); also (from its hardiness) a large lizard
H3581.kjv_def: ability, able, chameleon, force, fruits, might, power(-ful), strength, substance, wealth.
H3581.usage: strength(58x), power(47x), might(7x), force(3x), ability(2x), able(2x), chameleon(1x), fruits(1x), powerful(1x), substance(1x), wealth(1x)

H3582.strongs_def: to secrete, by act or word; hence (intensively) to destroy

H3584.strongs_def: to be untrue, in word (to lie, feign, disown) or deed (to disappoint, fail, cringe)
H3584.kjv_def: deceive, deny, dissemble, fail, deal falsely, be found liars, (be-) lie, lying, submit selves.
H3584.usage: lie(5x), submit(3x), deny(3x), fail(3x), denied(2x), belied(1x), deceive(1x), dissembled(1x), liars(1x), submitted(1x)

H3585.strongs_def: literally a failure of flesh, i.e. emaciation; figuratively, hypocrisy

H3588.derivation: a primitive particle (the full form of the prepositional prefix) indicating causal relations of all kinds, antecedent or consequent;
H3588.strongs_def: (by implication) very widely used as a relative conjunction or adverb (as below); often largely modified by other particles annexed
H3588.kjv_def: and, + (forasmuch, inasmuch, where-) as, assured(-ly), + but, certainly, doubtless, + else, even, + except, for, how, (because, in, so, than) that, + nevertheless, now, rightly, seeing, since, surely, then, therefore, + (al-) though, + till, truly, + until, when, whether, while, whom, yea, yet.

H3589.derivation: from a primitive root meaning to strike;

H3590.derivation: from the same as H3589 (כִּיד) (compare H3539 (כַּדְכֹּד));
H3590.strongs_def: properly, something struck off, i.e. a spark (as struck)

H3591.derivation: from the same as H3589 (כִּיד); (perhaps smaller than H2595 (חֲנִית)lemma חֲניִת second vowel, corrected to חֲנִית)
H3591.strongs_def: properly, something to strike with, i.e. a dart

H3594.strongs_def: properly, a statue, i.e. idol; but used (by euphemism) forsome heathen deity (perhaps corresponding to Priapus or Baal-peor)
H3594.pos: proper noun with reference to deity

H3595.derivation: or כִּיֹּר; from the same as H3564 (כּוּר);
H3595.strongs_def: properly, something round (as excavated or bored), i.e. a chafing-dish forcoals or a caldron forcooking; hence (from similarity of form) a washbowl; also (for the same reason) a pulpit or platform

H3596.derivation: or כֵּלַי; from H3557 (כּוּל) in the sense of withholding;

H3597.derivation: from an unused root meaning to clap or strike with noise;

H3598.strongs_def: a cluster of stars, i.e. the Pleiades

H3599.strongs_def: a cup; also a bag formoney or weights
